title: Phaeneilema paniculatum
taxonomy: Phaeneilema paniculatum is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the genus Phaeneilema and is further categorized as a species within the subfamily Eragrostoideae. The exact classification can be broken down as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: angiosperms, clade: monocots, order: Poales, family: Poaceae, genus: Phaeneilema, species: P. paniculatum. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and its evolutionary relationships to other species within the Poaceae family.

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
